attractions.name: Boulder City Historic District
attractions.description: Established in the 1930s as a model community for Hoover Dam workers, the Boulder City Historic District is the largest listing on the National Register of Historic Places in Nevada. The district features over 500 historic buildings showcasing period revival architectural styles like Spanish Colonial and Art Deco, offering a peaceful, historic escape from the Las Vegas Strip (National Park Service, Boulder City NV).

attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 7:00 PM (Boulder City/Hoover Dam Museum)

highlights: ['Historic Boulder Dam Hotel and Museum', 'Historic Boulder Theatre marquee', 'Frank T. Crowe Memorial Park and Murals', 'WALK BC self-guided audio tour', 'Charming antique shops and art galleries', 'Well-preserved Art Deco and Pueblo Revival architecture']
attractions.tips: Download the free WALK BC app for a narrated mile-long tour of the district's history. Visit Hemenway Park nearby at dawn or dusk for a high chance of seeing desert bighorn sheep grazing. The district is pedestrian-friendly, so wear comfortable walking shoes (Boulder City Chamber, Airial Travel).

It serves as a natural getaway that provides lush habitats for over 300 species of plants and animals while improving the local water quality. Visitors can explore miles of paved and unpaved trails, interactive exhibits at the Nature Center, and scenic views of the Las Vegas Wash. Clark County Nevada, Visit Las Vegas

attractions.name: Death Valley National Park
attractions.description: Death Valley National Park is a below-sea-level basin featuring steady drought and record summer heat, making it a land of extremes with towering snow-capped peaks and vast wildflower fields. As the hottest, driest, and lowest national park in the United States, it offers a striking contrast of landscapes including sand dunes, salt flats, and deep canyons (National Park Service).
attractions.address: California Highway 190, Death Valley, CA 92328 (GPS for Furnace Creek Visitor Center: N 36°27.70, W 116°52.00)
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours daily; Furnace Creek Visitor Center: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $30.00; Motorcycle: $25.00; Individual (on foot/bicycle): $15.00; Death Valley Annual Pass: $55.00; America the Beautiful Pass (Annual/Senior/Military/Access): Free-$80.00 (National Park Service)
highlights: 'Badwater Basin: The lowest point in North America at 282 feet below sea level, featuring vast salt flats ([National Park Service).', 'Zabriskie Point: A famous overlook offering panoramic views of colorful, eroded badlands, best viewed at sunrise or sunset (Johnny Africa).', 'Artists Palette: A scenic drive through multi-colored volcanic and sedimentary hills that appear painted in shades of pink, green, and purple (My Perfect Itinerary).', 'Mesquite Flat Sand Dunes: Expansive dunes that are easily accessible and offer a classic desert landscape experience (My Perfect Itinerary).', "Dante’s View: A mountain-top overlook at 5,475 feet providing a bird's-eye view of the entire valley floor (Gypsy With a Day Job)."]
attractions.tips: Plan to go cashless as the park only accepts credit/debit or digital payments for fees. Avoid hiking in summer months due to extreme heat, and always carry extra water and a physical map since GPS and cell service are unreliable (National Park Service, Johnny Africa).
attractions.nearby: Rhyolite Ghost Town, Ash Meadows National Wildlife Refuge, Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area, Valley of Fire State Park, and the Amargosa Opera House (National Park Service).

attractions.name: Dolby Live at Park MGM (Park MGM)

It is the world's first performance venue to feature a fully integrated Dolby Atmos sound system, offering an immersive audio experience for high-profile residencies and concerts. The venue features one of the widest stages in the world and provides exceptional sightlines, with the furthest seat being only 145 feet from the stage (Park MGM, Wikipedia).

attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically ranging from approximately $10 to over $7,000 for premium seating tiers, including Orchestra, High Orchestra, Balcony, and VIP Banquettes (Vivid Seats, Event Tickets Center)
highlights: "World's first fully integrated Dolby Atmos audio system with 402 speakers ([Park MGM)", "One of the world's widest stages at 140 feet across (Park MGM)", 'Intimate seating for 5,200 guests with sightlines no further than 145 feet (Park MGM)', 'Massive 240-foot wide projection surface with advanced mapping technology (Park MGM)', 'Seven bars and terraces offering scenic views of the Las Vegas Strip (Park MGM)']
attractions.tips: Strict no-bag policy except for small purses (12″x12″x6″ or smaller). The venue is entirely smoke-free, including all terraces. It is highly recommended to pre-pay for parking in the Park MGM garage for event nights to ensure availability (Park MGM).

attractions.name: Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours and Nelson Ghost Town
attractions.description: Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours is home to the historic Techatticup Mine, the oldest and richest gold mine in Southern Nevada, which operated from 1861 to 1942. The site features the "Nelson Ghost Town," a collection of weathered buildings, vintage vehicles, and movie relics that serve as a popular destination for history buffs and photographers. Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours, Travel Nevada

attractions.hours: Monday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Tuesday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Wednesday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Thursday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Friday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Saturday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Sunday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Note: Mine tours are typically scheduled for 9:00 AM, 12:00 PM, and 2:00 PM daily. TripAdvisor, Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week), except for Easter, Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve (closed at noon), and Christmas Day. Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours
attractions.admission: Mine Tours: $20.00 for adults (13 and older), $12.50 for kids (ages 5-12), Free for children under 5. Grounds access/photo shoots: Small fee required (approx. $1-$2 for general grounds access, $10-$20+ for organized photo sessions); check in at the general store for current rates. Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours, Yelp
highlights: ['Guided walking tours of the historic Techatticup Mine tunnels.', 'Extensive collection of vintage cars, trucks, and antique mining equipment.', "Famous movie filming locations, including the plane crash set from '3000 Miles to Graceland'.", 'Evocative "Wild West" ghost town atmosphere with weathered buildings and a general store museum.', 'Scenic views of Eldorado Canyon and proximity to the Colorado River.', 'Canoe and kayak rentals available for exploring the nearby Black Canyon.']
attractions.tips: Reservations are strictly required for mine tours, which need at least four adults to proceed. Wear sturdy, closed-toe walking shoes and bring water, as the terrain is gravel and the desert sun can be intense. The interior of the mine remains a cool 65 degrees year-round, so a light jacket may be needed even in summer. All photography, including personal shoots, requires checking in and paying a fee at the general store first. Eldorado Canyon Mine Tours, Las Vegas Photography Blog

attractions.name: Floyd Lamb Park at Tule Springs

The park is home to the historic Tule Springs Ranch and provides visitors with a unique look at early ranch life in the Las Vegas region (Travel Nevada).

attractions.hours: Summer (April – September): Monday–Sunday 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Winter (October – March): Monday–Sunday 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (City of Las Vegas)
attractions.admission: $6 per vehicle (Cashless: Debit or Credit only); Household Yearly Pass: $50 (ages through 49), Senior Household Yearly Pass: $25 (ages 50+); Free on select dates including May 15, July 4, and September 12 (City of Las Vegas)
highlights: 'Four spring-fed lakes (Tule Springs, Mulberry, Cottonwood, and Desert Willow) stocked with fish ([City of Las Vegas)', 'The historic Tule Springs Ranch, which offers a glimpse into early Las Vegas history (Travel Nevada)', 'Abundant wildlife including free-roaming peacocks, ducks, geese, and herons (City of Las Vegas)', 'Extensive trails for walking, jogging, horse riding, and mountain biking (City of Las Vegas)', 'Mountain bike pump track and volleyball courts for recreational use (City of Las Vegas)', 'Lush, shaded picnic areas equipped with barbecue grills (Travel Nevada)']
attractions.tips: Entry is cashless; ensure you have a debit or credit card to pay the vehicle fee. Feeding the wildlife, particularly the peacocks and waterfowl, is strictly prohibited. attractions.name: Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve (City of Henderson)
attractions.category: Park / Nature Reserve (City of Henderson, Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Henderson Bird Viewing Preserve is a 140-acre oasis home to thousands of migratory waterfowl and numerous resident desert birds. Located within the city's Water Reclamation Facility, it features nine tranquil ponds and five miles of trails, including a wheelchair-accessible paved path. This premier birding spot is recognized as one of the best locations for birdwatching in southern Nevada. (City of Henderson, Travel Nevada)
attractions.address: 350 E. Galleria Dr., Henderson, NV 89011 (City of Henderson)
attractions.hours: Open daily 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM; Summer Hours (June-August) 6:00 AM – 12:00 PM; Last entry 30 minutes before closing (City of Henderson)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Henderson, Travel Nevada)
highlights: 'Nine tranquil ponds across a 140-acre specialized habitat ([City of Henderson)', 'Five miles of trails, including a 3/4-mile wheelchair-accessible paved path (City of Henderson)', 'Home to over 270 species of resident and migratory birds (Travel Nevada)', "March's Monarch Garden featuring interpretive signs and a mural by artist Shan Michael Evans (City of Henderson)", 'Visitor Center with a gift shop and binoculars available for loan (City of Henderson)', 'Guided "Bird Mobile" cart tours and walking tours (City of Henderson)']
attractions.tips: All visitors must sign in at the Visitor Center; first-time visitors are required to sign a liability waiver. It is highly recommended to visit early in the morning when birds are most active and temperatures are cooler. Visitors should bring water, wear a hat and sunscreen, and use comfortable walking shoes. Note that pets, biking, and running are not permitted on the facility grounds. (City of Henderson, Travel Nevada)

attractions.name: High Roller Observation Wheel (Official Site)

As the largest observation wheel in North America, it features 28 climate-controlled cabins that take 30 minutes to complete a full rotation. It provides a unique perspective of Las Vegas landmarks, especially when illuminated at night (Official Site, Visit Las Vegas).

highlights: 'Stands at a massive 550 feet tall, making it the tallest observation wheel in North America ([Official Site)', 'Features 28 spacious, climate-controlled cabins that can hold up to 40 guests each (Official Site)', "Offers a 'Happy Half Hour' cabin option with a full open bar and bartender for guests 21+ (Official Site)", 'Provides a full 30-minute rotation for breathtaking 360-degree photo opportunities of the Strip (Official Site)', 'Wheel remains in motion throughout the day, allowing for continuous boarding (Coasterpedia))', 'Host unique events including sky-high weddings and corporate parties (Official Site)']

attractions.name: Hoover Dam
attractions.description: Hoover Dam is a concrete arch-gravity dam in the Black Canyon of the Colorado River, on the border between Nevada and Arizona. Constructed between 1931 and 1936, it is a National Historic Landmark and an engineering marvel that impounds Lake Mead, the largest reservoir in the United States by volume. Wikipedia, Bureau of Reclamation
attractions.address: 81 Hoover Dam Access Road
attractions.hours: Hoover Dam (Public Access): 5:00 a.m. – 9:00 p.m. daily. Visitor Center and Tours: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. daily (Doors close at 4:15 p.m.). Bureau of Reclamation
attractions.days_open: Daily (Visitor Center and Tours closed on Thanksgiving and Christmas Day) Bureau of Reclamation
attractions.admission: Guided Dam Tour: $40 per person (Children 3 and under: Free; Purchase on-site only). Guided Power Plant Tour: $25 Adults, $15 Ages 4-16, Children 3 and under Free. Self-Guided Visitor Center Tour: $15 per person, Children 3 and under Free. Bureau of Reclamation
highlights: ['Guided tours of the dam and power plant tunnels', 'Panoramic views from the Observation Deck over Lake Mead and the Colorado River', 'Historical exhibits and a narrated 3D model in the Visitor Center', 'Walk across the top of the dam between Nevada and Arizona', "View of the Mike O'Callaghan-Pat Tillman Memorial Bridge", 'Art Deco architectural details and Winged Figures of the Republic statues']
attractions.tips: Arrive early as Guided Dam Tour tickets are sold only on-site and frequently sell out. Be prepared for multiple security checkpoints and vehicle inspections. Service animals are welcome, but pets and comfort animals are not allowed on the dam or in buildings. Dress for outdoor weather and wear comfortable walking shoes. Bureau of Reclamation

attractions.name: Horseshoe Bend
attractions.description: Horseshoe Bend is a spectacular horseshoe-shaped meander of the Colorado River featuring a dramatic 1,000-foot drop to the emerald-green waters below. Located within the Glen Canyon National Recreation Area, it offers panoramic views of the red sandstone cliffs and is one of the most iconic natural landmarks in the American Southwest. The site is accessible via a 1.5-mile round-trip trail and is a popular day-trip destination for visitors traveling from Las Vegas or the Grand Canyon (Official Site, NPS).
attractions.address: Mile Marker 545, Highway 89, Page, AZ 86040
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset (Official Site)
attractions.admission: $10 per passenger vehicle (car, truck, SUV, RV), $5 per motorcycle, $35 for commercial vans/buses (up to 14 capacity), $70 for commercial buses (15-35 capacity), $140 for commercial buses (35+ capacity) (Official Site)
highlights: 'Spectacular 1,000-foot vertical drop with panoramic river views ([Official Site)', 'Iconic horseshoe-shaped meander carved by the Colorado River (NPS)', '1.5-mile round-trip ADA-compliant hiking trail (Official Site)', 'Exceptional sunrise and sunset photography opportunities (Visit Arizona)', 'Unique red sandstone geology and desert landscape within Glen Canyon NRA (NPS)']
attractions.tips: Bring at least one quart of water per person and wear sunscreen as there is minimal shade on the trail (Official Site). Wear sturdy, closed-toe footwear for the sandy and rocky terrain. Arrive early or late in the day to avoid peak heat and crowds, and be extremely cautious near the rim as much of the overlook is unfenced (NPS). Drones are strictly prohibited (Official Site).

The exhibition features seven towering totems of colorful, stacked boulders that stand more than 30 feet high against the desert landscape. It serves as a creative critique of human presence in the desert, symbolically situated midway between the natural surroundings and the artificial environment of the nearby highway and city. Official Website

Official Website', 'Site-specific installation produced by the Nevada Museum of Art and Art Production Fund. Official Website', 'Offers an interactive audio tour available via a Guide-By-Cell offering. Official Website']
attractions.tips: Visit during sunrise or sunset for cooler temperatures and the best photographic lighting. Wear sturdy, closed-toe shoes and stay on the walking paths due to potential desert wildlife like rattlesnakes. Bring sun protection and plenty of water, especially during summer months. Its otherworldly landscape, which appears to glow at sunset, makes it a popular destination for hiking, photography, and film production (Valley of Fire Website).

Housed in a historic 1933 federal courthouse and post office, it provides an insider's view of the ongoing battle between the Mob and law enforcement. Visitors can explore the complex relationship between criminals and the law through interactive experiences like a crime lab, firearm training simulator, and an authentic underground speakeasy. The Mob Museum Official Site, Visit Las Vegas

The plaza serves as a destination for authentic Asian cuisine, unique specialty shops, and cultural events such as the annual Lunar New Year celebration.
